verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type-1 transmembrane protein that serves as a Ca2+ sensor in the ER membrane. The canonical form is ER-resident with an N-terminal SAM domain in the ER lumen and a single-pass TM anchor. However, STIM1 has well-documented ER-to-PM trafficking and PM localization upon ER Ca2+ depletion — a hallmark activation-induced surface display mechanism. Upon store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes, translocates to ER-PM junctions, and directly couples to Orai1 channels at the PM to gate store-operated Ca2+ entry. This activation-induced PM translocation is a canonical example of cell-state-induced dual localization. The surface pool is functionally critical for SOCE signaling, qualifying STIM1 as contextually surface-accessible despite baseline ER residence.
